title: Nutrient/EC check: high root-zone and drainage EC

recommendations.reason: root-zone or drainage EC is high, feed EC is above the starting Seolhyang range, compare root-zone EC with drainage EC before changing fertigation
recommendations.expected_effect: check salinity accumulation or nutrient dilution before adjusting fertilizer strength
risks: do not diagnose plant nutrient status from EC alone

recommendations.reason: substrate moisture is below the literature starting threshold
recommendations.expected_effect: maintain root-zone water supply without treating the output as a control command
risks: confirm recent irrigation, drainage, and sensor units before changing volume
recent irrigation history should reduce urgency until response is checked

recommendations.expected_effect: reduce humidity and canopy wetness conditions while staying in decision-support mode
risks: human review is required before ventilation or dehumidification control changes

recommendations.expected_effect: review heat-stress mitigation without reducing photosynthesis unnecessarily
risks: excess shading can reduce photosynthesis and delay ripening

recommendations.expected_effect: review heating or thermal protection for flowering, fruiting, and ripening stability
risks: consider energy cost and humidity rise when vents are closed

what_if_simulation.summary: v0 what-if scenario estimate for decision support; compare directions, not precise effects
what_if_simulation.not_validated_warning: This is a what-if scenario estimate, not a validated causal simulation or control command.

expected_state_direction: EC issue remains unresolved until fertigation is adjusted
potential_benefits: salinity or dilution risk may be identified before nutrient changes
potential_risks: checking alone does not correct EC

evidence_references.title: Sensor-controlled fertigation management for higher yield and quality in greenhouse hydroponic strawberries; Nutrient solution strength studies for Korean strawberry cultivars
evidence_references.note: Local markdown: 01_s13580-025-00677-z.md describes EC monitoring and 0.6 to 1.5 dS/m management; 06_fpls-16-1685755.md summarizes cultivar-specific EC ranges and need for ongoing EC/ion monitoring.

assumptions: directional v0 estimate based on literature/manual rules
local calibration and human review are required
scenarios.not_validated_warning: This is a what-if scenario estimate, not a validated causal simulation or control command.

expected_state_direction: EC issue may dilute only if drainage is adequate
potential_benefits: water-stress risk may decrease during high VPD or radiation
potential_risks: over-wet substrate risk if recent irrigation is ignored

evidence_references.title: Sensor-controlled fertigation management for higher yield and quality in greenhouse hydroponic strawberries; Solar-radiation and substrate-moisture fertigation study for Seolhyang strawberry
evidence_references.note: Prototype/manual-review-needed rule. Local markdown: 01_s13580-025-00677-z.md links Rad/VPD/LAI to transpiration; 08_N0090300416.md reports solar-radiation and substrate-moisture fertigation including 60% moisture and 100/150/250 J/cm2 treatments.

assumptions: directional v0 estimate based on literature/manual rules
local calibration and human review are required
